title: festuca ovina subsp. elliptica
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. elliptica, also known as a type of grass in the Poaceae family, specifically a subspecies of sheep's fescue, is a plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Europe. The medicinal properties of Festuca ovina subsp. elliptica include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are obtained from its essential oil. The health benefits of using this plant include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating wounds and skin irritations. Festuca ovina subsp. elliptica can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, where its essential oil is typically extracted and added to carrier oils for topical applications.
